text: Lobotectomy and mediastinal lymphotectomy were performed.
Three operating pods were used during the single direction video assisted thoracoscopic lobectomy.
The mediastinal lymph nodes were dissected firstly before the lobectomy because the pathological result was confirmed by EBUS-TPNA before the neoadjuvant targeted therapy.
The left inferior pulmonary ligaments were dissected and the 9th lymph nodes were resected.
The next is the dissection of the 10th P lymph nodes while the pulmonary artery was exposed.
The next step was the dissection of subcoronal lymph nodes, seventh lymph nodes.
The ebus TBNA to metastatic subcoronal lymph nodes was performed eight weeks ago.
It means that it was important to dissect its end block.
Exposure is difficult in the dissection of subcoronal lymph nectomy, so the assistant inserted a forester's forcep into the auxiliary ports to expose the narrow space where the lymph nodes located.
And then the pleural cavity should be observed in the stable way.
The chief isolated the edge close to the right primary bronchus by a ultrasound scalpel.
The unnecessary injury to the normal organs such as bronchus, esophagus, and artery should be avoided.
Then the chief isolated the edge close to the precardium.
At last, the chief isolated the edge close to the right primary bronchus.
In this way, the lymph nodes can be resected and blocked.
We finished the dissection of the subcoronal lymph nodes.
The following step was the dissection of the fourth lymph nodes.
This LN located in the potential space among the trachea, the left primary bronchus, left pulmonary artery, and aorta.
The key of this step was the exposure to the space by the assistant.
The injury to the left pulmonary artery should be avoided.
We turn to resect the 10th LN after finishing the dissection of the 4th LN.
As you can see, the root of the left pulmonary artery can be observed.
The 5th LN is close to the 10th LN.
The assistant lifted the LN and the chief dissected it without unnecessary injury.
Return to resect the sixth LN after finishing the dissection of the fifth LN.
The sixth LN is close to the phrenic nerve aorta.
The assistant pulled the lymph nodes and the chief dissected it completely.
After we finished the dissection of the 9th, 10th, 4th, 10th, 5th, 6th lymph nodes, we started to do the lobectomy in the single direction method due to the fused oblique fissure.
For traditional left lobectomy, the following structures are operated in the order pulmonary vein,
oblique fissure pulmonary artery poorly developed fissure bronchus in this case the following structure are operated in this order pulmonary vein bronchus pulmonary artery fuse oblique fissure
The assistant inserted the forester forceps into the auxiliary port to track stumps of the left inferior vein towards the head site, so as to expose the left inferior bronchus.
The chief isolated the hessian between the 12 lymph nodes and the left inferior bronchus.
The chief dissected the 12 lymph nodes and then isolated the ischium between the left inferior bronchus and the left inferior artery.
We should avoid injury to the primary artery.
At last, the chief dissected the bronchus using the endosrete cutting clothes.
The assistant went on to insert a forested forcep into the ulcerary port to lift stump of the left inferior bronchus towards the head side, so as to expose the left inferior artery.
The chief isolated the branches of the left inferior pulmonary artery, including the basal artery and the dorsal artery.
The chief dissected the basal artery using the endostrate cutting clothes.
The dorsal artery is quite short, and the chief cut dorsal artery with silk and hemlock.
All the structures are dissected.
We should remove the 11th LM before cutting fused oblique fissure.
The single direction video assisted thoracoscopic lobectomy are operated in this order.
Pulmonary vein broadcast, pulmonary artery, fused oblique fissure.
The cutting of the 11th LM was so important that we should expose the branches of the left superior pulmonary artery, such as A4 plus 5, A1 plus 2C, and then dissected the 11th lymph nodes radically.
The last step was the cutting of the fused oblique fissure.
The chief cut the fissure with an endosurge cutting stapler and the navel patch.
The navel patch was used to prevent the postoperative air leak.
The patient was discharged without air leak at the 5th postoperative days.
The branches of the left superior pulmonary artery should not be injured accidentally in this procedure.
The radical dissection of the 11th lymph nodes should be performed.
The specimen was removed.
A chest tube was placed through the assistant port.
The stumps were illustrated as it is demonstrated here.
